core_mechanism: a quadratically-convergent Newton iteration that solves an invariance (fixed-point) functional equation whose linearized correction step requires inverting a near-resonant operator with small divisors, combined with pseudo-arclength continuation to trace the solution branch through fold (turning-point) singularities
brief_reason: The abstract states the approach is the parameterization method of KAM theory framed as 'a Newton scheme with small divisors' that solves for an invariant object, made amenable to pseudo-arclength continuation to traverse saddle-node bifurcations.
